document.name: microservice-dev-session
document.title: Microservice dev-session: provision a live dev preview, then hand off to an interactive coding agent
document.summary: Runs the five catalog-backed workflow-builder microservices (workflow-builder, workflow-orchestrator, function-router, mcp-gateway, and workflow-mcp-server) in an isolated preview-native vCluster development environment. It clones one immutable source revision into /sandbox/work, writes the multi-service sync helper, then hands the shared workspace and live preview to a persistent interactive coding-agent session for hot-reload development and full-system inspection.

service.title: Microservice to develop
service.description: workflow-builder | workflow-orchestrator | function-router | mcp-gateway | workflow-mcp-server (catalog-backed preview services).

mode.title: Dev-preview mode
mode.description: preview-native runs inside an isolated Tier-2 vCluster and adopts each selected service for live edits. host-throwaway remains an explicit compatibility mode for legacy host-cluster sessions.

keepPreview.title: Keep the preview alive after the run

artifacts.from: ${ { service: (.data.service // ""), url: (.data.url // ""), browseUrl: (.data.browseUrl // ""), syncUrl: (.data.syncUrl // ""), podIP: (.data.podIP // ""), ready: (.data.ready // false) } }
artifacts.title: Per-run dev preview

with.command:
${ "export REPOURL=" + ((.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/workflow-builder") | @sh) + "; export SOURCE_REVISION=" + ((.trigger.sourceRevision // "") | @sh) + "; export MODE=" + ((.trigger.mode // "preview-native") | @sh) + "; export PREVIEWS_B64=" + (((.provision_preview.services // [{service: (.provision_preview.service // .trigger.service // "workflow-orchestrator"), ok: (.provision_preview.ready // false), info: .provision_preview}]) | @json | @base64) | @sh) + ";" + "
set -eu
cd /sandbox/work
rm -rf repo .syncenv .syncenv.d .preview-services.json
printf '%s' "$REPOURL" | grep -Eq '^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$' || { echo 'repoUrl must be owner/repo' >&2; exit 2; }
case "$MODE" in host-throwaway|preview-native) ;; *) echo 'unsupported dev-preview mode' >&2; exit 2 ;; esac
if [ "$MODE" = preview-native ]; then printf '%s' "$SOURCE_REVISION" | grep -Eq '^[0-9a-f]{40}$' || { echo 'preview-native requires a lowercase 40-hex sourceRevision' >&2; exit 2; }; fi
if [ -n "${GITHUB_TOKEN:-}" ]; then CLONE_URL="https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/$REPOURL.git"; else CLONE_URL="https://github.com/$REPOURL.git"; fi
git clone --filter=blob:none "$CLONE_URL" repo
if [ -n "$SOURCE_REVISION" ]; then printf '%s' "$SOURCE_REVISION" | grep -Eq '^[0-9a-f]{40}$' || { echo 'sourceRevision must be a lowercase 40-hex Git SHA' >&2; exit 2; }; git -C repo fetch --no-tags --depth 1 origin "$SOURCE_REVISION"; git -C repo checkout --detach "$SOURCE_REVISION"; ACTUAL_REVISION=$(git -C repo rev-parse HEAD); test "$ACTUAL_REVISION" = "$SOURCE_REVISION" || { echo 'checked-out revision does not match sourceRevision' >&2; exit 3; }; fi
printf '%s' "$PREVIEWS_B64" | base64 -d > .preview-services.json
jq -e 'length > 0 and all(.[]; .ok == true and .info.ready == true and ((.info.syncUrl // "") | length) > 0)' .preview-services.json >/dev/null || { echo 'full preview service set is not ready' >&2; exit 4; }
mkdir -p .syncenv.d
jq -c '.[] | select(.ok == true)' .preview-services.json | while IFS= read -r entry; do service=$(printf '%s' "$entry" | jq -r '.service'); printf '%s' "$entry" | jq -r '.service as $service | (.info // .) as $info | "SERVICE=\($service | @sh)\nSUBDIR=\(($info.repoSubdir // ".") | @sh)\nPATHS=\((($info.syncPaths // ["src"]) | join(" ")) | @sh)\nSYNCURL=\(($info.syncUrl // "") | @sh)\nSYNC_TOKEN=\(($info.syncCapability // "") | @sh)\nEXTRASYNC=\(((($info.extraSync // []) + ($info.captureOnly // [])) | map(.from + ":" + .to) | join(" ")) | @sh)"' > ".syncenv.d/$service"; done
cp repo/scripts/dev-sync/sync.sh /sandbox/work/sync.sh
chmod +x /sandbox/work/sync.sh
echo CLONED $(git -C repo rev-parse HEAD)
echo SERVICES $(jq -r '[.[] | select(.ok == true) | .service] | join(",")' .preview-services.json)" }

with.instructions:
${ "You are the interactive developer for these microservices: **" + ((.trigger.services // (if .trigger.service then [.trigger.service] else ["workflow-builder", "workflow-orchestrator", "function-router", "mcp-gateway", "workflow-mcp-server"] end)) | join(", ")) + "**.

- One monorepo checkout is at /sandbox/work/repo. The per-service source mappings are in /sandbox/work/.syncenv.d/.
- The full isolated system is already running. Edit any selected service in that checkout, then run /sandbox/work/sync.sh once; it pushes one logical generation to every selected service and hot-reloads UI and backend processes.
- sync.sh detects dependency-manifest changes and runs the cataloged dependency action. Cataloged in-pod tests are available through each service's /__run endpoint. After syncing a workflow-builder drizzle/ change, run its allowlisted migrate action before testing the live schema.
- Run the relevant fast tests after edits, then inspect the functioning system at " + (.trigger.previewOrigin // "the provisioned browse URL") + ". Exercise cross-service behavior, not only a standalone health route.
- Do not commit or push from the session. Atomic capture and PR promotion are separate workflow actions.

Reply 'ready' and wait for the requested feature. Then implement it across the selected services, run sync.sh, run the allowlisted tests, and verify the live system." }
